name: Prg, Inc.
description_short: Minneapolis-based nonprofit providing free HUD-approved housing counseling, first-time homebuyer education, and foreclosure prevention to eliminate racial disparities in homeownership.
description_long:
PRG, Inc. has operated as a community-based nonprofit since 1976, based in Minneapolis. The organization combines affordable housing development with financial education and one-on-one advising to address systemic barriers in homeownership, particularly for BIPOC communities. Their mission explicitly targets racial disparities in homeownership and neighborhood stability.

PRG offers a comprehensive suite of free and low-cost services for both prospective and existing homeowners. Services include one-on-one first-time homebuyer advising, group workshops (Home Stretch program) with waived registration fees, HUD-approved foreclosure prevention consulting, homeowner education workshops, and refinance advising. They also partner with Framework to offer an online first-time homebuyer course for $75. Additionally, PRG develops and sells affordable homes in Minneapolis targeted at BIPOC communities to increase homeownership rates.

What distinguishes PRG is its integrated approach combining housing counseling with actual affordable housing development and inventory. The organization serves predominantly BIPOC, first-time, and first-generation homebuyers—reaching nearly 1,500 households in 2024, with approximately 220 becoming homeowners. Their foreclosure prevention consulting is conducted by HUD-approved advisors at no cost. The organization's longevity (48+ years) and board leadership from program participants (evidenced by client testimonials) suggests sustained community trust and institutional stability.

For consumers seeking homeownership education and foreclosure prevention help, PRG represents a legitimate nonprofit resource. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—services are Minneapolis-based, limiting accessibility for those outside the region. Additionally, while their first-time homebuyer workshops are free, the online alternative carries a $75 fee, and actual home purchases depend on PRG's affordable housing inventory availability in their development areas.
pros: HUD-approved foreclosure prevention advisors offering free confidential consultations
First-time homebuyer workshops (Home Stretch program) have waived registration fees due to sponsor support
One-on-one personalized advising for mortgage preparation and homeownership planning
Integrated affordable housing development—PRG both educates and builds homes for BIPOC communities
Nearly 1,500 households served in 2024 with 220 achieving homeownership, demonstrating measurable impact
48+ year track record since 1976 with documented client success stories and board-level engagement
Multiple service formats including in-person, group workshops, and online options ($75 Framework course)
cons: Geographically limited to Minneapolis area—services not accessible to consumers in other regions
Online first-time homebuyer course carries $75 fee, creating cost barrier despite free in-person workshops
Affordable home inventory depends on PRG's development capacity—not all clients may find available properties
Website content does not clearly specify income limits, credit score requirements, or eligibility criteria for programs
Foreclosure prevention effectiveness dependent on individual circumstances; counseling cannot guarantee loan modifications or prevent foreclosure in all cases
best_for: First-time BIPOC homebuyers in Minneapolis seeking free education and personalized mortgage preparation
Minneapolis homeowners facing foreclosure seeking free HUD-approved counseling and options review
Prospective homebuyers wanting to purchase affordable, community-developed housing in Minneapolis
Long-time homeowners seeking refinance guidance and continued homeownership education
diagnosis: PRG is best for first-time and existing homeowners in the Minneapolis area seeking free HUD-certified housing counseling, foreclosure prevention advice, and homebuyer education—particularly BIPOC and first-generation buyers. The primary caveat is geographic limitation; their services are Minneapolis-based and not remotely accessible to consumers in other regions.
services: First-time homebuyer group workshops—Home Stretch program (free, waived registration)
Online first-time homebuyer course through Framework partnership ($75)
HUD-approved foreclosure prevention consulting (free and confidential)
Homeowner group education workshops (ongoing education for stability)
Affordable home sales in Minneapolis for BIPOC communities
Educational programs for prospective homebuyers on homeownership process
Housing counseling addressing racial disparities in homeownership
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
